title: Pack: gezellig
subtitle: Warm-up → meaning → usage → your line → Talk

config.englishMeaning: Warm, sociable atmosphere — good company. Not “efficient” or “strictly formal”.
config.shortUsageNote: People, evenings, cafés, homes: “het was gezellig” is everyday Dutch.

exampleLines.english: It was really nice last night (good company).

exampleLines.dutch: Zullen we ergens gezellig gaan zitten?
exampleLines.english: Shall we sit somewhere relaxed / cosy?

exampleLines.dutch: Ik vond het café erg gezellig.
exampleLines.english: I found the café really cosy / friendly.

blocks.instruction: Pick the closest meaning in real life.

config.prompt: What does “gezellig” usually communicate in conversation?

options.label: Only used for bad weather warnings

config.correctExplanation: Gezellig is about warmth and company — not efficiency.

config.hint: Use device audio — listen once, repeat twice.

config.prompt: Which usage fits everyday Dutch best?

options.label: Het was heel gezellig — lekker bijgepraat.
Het was heel gezellig — dus we werkten snel door.
Het was gezellig — ik vond het te duur.
Gezellig — dus ik wilde meteen naar huis.

config.correctExplanation: Gezellig pairs with social warmth, not “we worked fast”.

config.prompt: Write one Dutch sentence with “gezellig” about your real day (not copied from the examples).

config.instruction: Say your new sentence out loud twice — relaxed shoulders.

config.ctaLabel: Open Talk for a short live rep
config.reason: When you want feedback on tone and word choice, a live drill helps.
